1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"

20	
21	// ReviewVerdict is the outcome of a post-merge bead review.
22	type ReviewVerdict string
23	
24	const (
25		// VerdictApprove means all AC items passed; the bead stays closed.
26		VerdictApprove ReviewVerdict = "APPROVE"
27		// VerdictRequestChanges means some AC items need fixing; the bead is reopened.
28		VerdictRequestChanges ReviewVerdict = "REQUEST_CHANGES"
29		// VerdictBlock means escalation should stop; the bead is flagged for human review.
30		VerdictBlock ReviewVerdict = "BLOCK"
31	)
32	
33	// ReviewResult is the structured outcome of a post-merge bead review.
34	type ReviewResult struct {
35		Verdict         ReviewVerdict `json:"verdict"`
36		Rationale       string        `json:"rationale,omitempty"`
37		PerAC           []ReviewAC    `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
38		RawOutput       string        `json:"raw_output,omitempty"`
39		ReviewerHarness string        `json:"reviewer_harness,omitempty"`
40		ReviewerModel   string        `json:"reviewer_model,omitempty"`
41		SessionID       string        `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
42		BaseRev         string        `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
43		ResultRev       string        `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
44		ExecutionDir    string        `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
45		DurationMS      int           `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
46		Error           string        `json:"error,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	type ReviewAC struct {
50		Number   int    `json:"number"`
51		Item     string `json:"item,omitempty"`
52		Grade    string `json:"grade,omitempty"`
53		Evidence string `json:"evidence,omitempty"`
54	}
55	
56	type reviewArtifactManifest struct {
57		Harness      string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
58		Model        string `json:"model,omitempty"`
59		SessionID    string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
60		BaseRev      string `json:"base_rev,omitempty"`
61		ResultRev    string `json:"result_rev,omitempty"`
62		Verdict      string `json:"verdict,omitempty"`
63		BeadID       string `json:"bead_id,omitempty"`
64		ExecutionDir string `json:"execution_dir,omitempty"`
65	}
66	
67	type reviewArtifactResult struct {
68		Verdict   string     `json:"verdict"`
69		PerAC     []ReviewAC `json:"per_ac,omitempty"`
70		Rationale string     `json:"rationale,omitempty"`

72	}
73	
74	// reVerdictLine matches "### Verdict: APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK"
75	// anywhere in the output, case-insensitive, allowing 1–4 leading hashes.
76	var reVerdictLine = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`)
77	var reReviewSectionHeading = regexp.MustCompile(`(?im)^#{1,4}\s+(AC Grades|Summary|Findings)\s*:?\s*$`)
78	var reACReference = regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)\bAC#\s*([0-9]+)\b`)
79	
80	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable is returned by ParseReviewVerdictStrict when
81	// the reviewer output does not contain a recognizable `### Verdict: ...`
82	// line. Callers should surface this as a review-error (retryable) rather
83	// than silently treating it as BLOCK — the pre-ddx-f7ae036f behavior was to
84	// default-to-BLOCK, which produced spurious APPROVE→BLOCK mis-records
85	// whenever the reviewer's output shape drifted (codex stream frames,
86	// rationale-only responses, stall-truncated output).
87	var ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable = fmt.Errorf("reviewer output: no recognizable verdict line")
88	
89	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict is the structured-error variant of
90	// ParseReviewVerdict: on unparseable input it returns
91	// ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable instead of silently returning VerdictBlock.
92	// The execute-loop uses this path so a parse failure reopens the bead for
93	// retry rather than recording a false BLOCK.
94	func ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output string) (ReviewVerdict, error) {
95		m := reVerdictLine.FindStringSubmatch(output)
96		if m == nil {
97			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
98		}
99		switch strings.ToUpper(strings.TrimSpace(m[1])) {
100		case "APPROVE":
101			return VerdictApprove, nil
102		case "REQUEST_CHANGES":
103			return VerdictRequestChanges, nil
104		case "BLOCK":
105			return VerdictBlock, nil
106		default:
107			return "", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable
108		}
109	}
110	
111	// ParseReviewVerdict extracts the verdict from a review agent's output.
112	// The expected format includes a line: ### Verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK
113	//
114	// Deprecated: returns VerdictBlock on unparseable input, which masks
115	// legitimate verdict-parse failures as BLOCKs and caused the wave-2
116	// review-malfunction incident (ddx-f7ae036f). New callers should use
117	// ParseReviewVerdictStrict and treat ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable as a
118	// retryable review-error.
119	func ParseReviewVerdict(output string) ReviewVerdict {
120		v, err := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
121		if err != nil {
122			return VerdictBlock
123		}
124		return v
125	}
126	
127	func ParseReviewResult(output string) (ReviewVerdict, []ReviewAC, string) {
128		verdict := ParseReviewVerdict(output)
129		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
130		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
131		if len(perAC) == 0 {
132			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict, rationale)
133		}
134		return verdict, perAC, rationale
135	}
136	
137	func parseReviewACTable(output string) []ReviewAC {
138		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
139		var rows []ReviewAC
140		inTable := false
141		for _, raw := range lines {
142			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
143			if line == "" {
144				if inTable {
145					break
146				}
147				continue
148			}
149			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
150				inTable = strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(line), "ac grades")
151				continue
152			}
153			if !inTable || !strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
154				continue
155			}
156			cols := splitMarkdownTableRow(line)
157			if len(cols) < 4 {
158				continue
159			}
160			if cols[0] == "#" || strings.HasPrefix(cols[0], "---") {
161				continue
162			}
163			n, err := strconv.Atoi(cols[0])
164			if err != nil {
165				continue
166			}
167			rows = append(rows, ReviewAC{
168				Number:   n,
169				Item:     cols[1],
170				Grade:    cols[2],
171				Evidence: cols[3],
172			})
173		}
174		return rows
175	}

177	func splitMarkdownTableRow(line string) []string {
178		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
179		out := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
180		for _, part := range parts {
181			part = strings.TrimSpace(part)
182			if part == "" {
183				continue
184			}
185			out = append(out, part)
186		}
187		return out

190	func extractReviewRationale(output string, perAC []ReviewAC) string {
191		lines := strings.Split(output, "\n")
192		var summaryLines []string
193		var findingLines []string
194		var section string
195		for _, raw := range lines {
196			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
197			if line == "" {
198				continue
199			}
200			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "## Review:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "### Verdict:") || strings.HasPrefix(line, "#### Verdict:") {
201				continue
202			}
203			if reReviewSectionHeading.MatchString(line) {
204				heading := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimLeft(line, "# "), ":")))
205				switch heading {
206				case "summary":
207					section = "summary"
208				case "findings":
209					section = "findings"
210				case "ac grades":
211					section = "ac"
212				default:
213					section = ""
214				}
215				continue
216			}
217			if section == "ac" && strings.HasPrefix(line, "|") {
218				continue
219			}
220			switch section {
221			case "findings":
222				line = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "-"))
223				if line != "" {
224					findingLines = append(findingLines, line)
225				}
226			case "summary":
227				summaryLines = append(summaryLines, line)
228			}
229		}
230		if len(findingLines) > 0 {
231			return strings.Join(findingLines, "\n")
232		}
233		if len(summaryLines) > 0 {
234			return strings.Join(summaryLines, "\n")
235		}
236		var unmet []string
237		for _, ac := range perAC {
238			if strings.EqualFold(ac.Grade, string(VerdictApprove)) {
239				continue
240			}
241			label := fmt.Sprintf("AC#%d", ac.Number)
242			if ac.Item != "" {
243				label += " " + ac.Item
244			}
245			if ac.Evidence != "" {
246				unmet = append(unmet, label+": "+ac.Evidence)
247			} else {
248				unmet = append(unmet, label)
249			}
250		}
251		return strings.Join(unmet, "\n")
252	}

254	func synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(verdict ReviewVerdict, rationale string) []ReviewAC {
255		if rationale == "" {
256			return nil
257		}
258		matches := reACReference.FindAllStringSubmatch(rationale, -1)
259		if len(matches) == 0 {
260			return nil
261		}
262		seen := make(map[int]struct{}, len(matches))
263		out := make([]ReviewAC, 0, len(matches))
264		for _, match := range matches {
265			if len(match) < 2 {
266				continue
267			}
268			n, err := strconv.Atoi(match[1])
269			if err != nil {
270				continue
271			}
272			if _, ok := seen[n]; ok {
273				continue
274			}
275			seen[n] = struct{}{}
276			out = append(out, ReviewAC{
277				Number:   n,
278				Grade:    string(verdict),
279				Evidence: rationale,
280			})
281		}
282		return out
283	}

361	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation.
362	// It renders the bead metadata, governing document contents, git diff, and
363	// review instructions into an XML-structured prompt string.
364	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
365		var sb strings.Builder
366	
367		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
368	
369		// ── Bead section ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
370		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
371		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
372	
373		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
374			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
375		} else {
376			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
377		}
378	
379		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
380			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
381		} else {
382			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
383		}
384	
385		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
386			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
387		}
388	
389		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
390	
391		// ── Governing docs section ───────────────────────────────────────────────
392		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
393		if len(refs) == 0 {
394			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
395		} else {
396			for _, ref := range refs {
397				if ref.Title != "" {
398					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
399				} else {
400					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
401				}
402				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
403				content, readErr := os.ReadFile(docPath)
404				if readErr != nil {
405					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
406				} else {
407					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", strings.TrimSpace(string(content)))
408				}
409				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
410			}
411		}
412		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
413	
414		// ── Diff section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
415		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(diff, "\n"))
416	
417		// ── Instructions section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
418		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
419		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
420		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
421	
422		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
423	
424		return sb.String()
425	}
426	
427	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
428	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
429		var buf bytes.Buffer
430		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
431			"&", "&",
432			"<", "<",
433			">", ">",
434		).Replace(s))
435		return buf.String()
436	}

438	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
439	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
440	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
441	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
442		ProjectRoot string
443		BeadStore   BeadReader
444		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.DdxAgent used to dispatch the
445		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
446		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
447		Service agentlib.DdxAgent
448		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
449		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
450		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
451		Runner AgentRunner
452		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
453		// When empty, Harness defaults to "claude" and Model is resolved
454		// from TierSmart for the chosen harness.

459	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
460	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev, implHarness, _ string) (*ReviewResult, error) {
461		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
462		if err != nil {
463			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
464		}
465	
466		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
467		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
468		if err != nil {
469			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
470		}
471	
472		// Resolve governing document references.
473		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
474	
475		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
476		iter := 1
477	
478		// Build the review prompt.
479		prompt := BuildReviewPrompt(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs)
480		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
481		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
482		if err != nil {
483			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
484		}
485		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
486			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
487		}
488	
489		// Resolve reviewer harness and model.
490		reviewHarness := r.Harness
491		if reviewHarness == "" {
492			if implHarness != "" {
493				reviewHarness = implHarness
494			} else {
495				reviewHarness = "claude" // default reviewer harness
496			}
497		}
498		reviewModel := r.Model
499		if reviewModel == "" {
500			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
501		}
502	
503		start := time.Now()
504		runOpts := RunOptions{
505			Context: ctx,
506			Harness: reviewHarness,
507			Model:   reviewModel,
508			Prompt:  prompt,
509			WorkDir: r.ProjectRoot,
510		}
511		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runOpts)
512	
513		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
514		if runErr != nil {
515			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
516				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
517				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
518				Error:           runErr.Error(),
519				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
520				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
521				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
522				ResultRev:       resultRev,
523				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
524				DurationMS:      durationMS,
525			}
526			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
527				Harness:      reviewHarness,
528				Model:        reviewModel,
529				BaseRev:      reviewRes.BaseRev,
530				ResultRev:    resultRev,
531				Verdict:      string(reviewRes.Verdict),
532				BeadID:       beadID,
533				ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
534			}, reviewArtifactResult{
535				Verdict:   string(reviewRes.Verdict),
536				Rationale: reviewRes.Rationale,
537				Error:     reviewRes.Error,
538			})
539			return reviewRes, nil
540		}
541	
542		actualHarness := reviewHarness
543		actualModel := reviewModel
544		if result != nil {
545			if result.Harness != "" {
546				actualHarness = result.Harness
547			}
548			if result.Model != "" {
549				actualModel = result.Model
550			}
551			durationMS = result.DurationMS
552		}
553	
554		output := ""
555		sessionID := ""
556		if result != nil {
557			output = result.Output
558			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
559		}
560		// Strict parse: unparseable reviewer output surfaces as a typed error so
561		// the execute-loop can classify it as review-error (retryable) rather
562		// than mis-recording it as BLOCK (ddx-f7ae036f). The review result
563		// artifacts still land on disk for forensics via writeReviewArtifacts
564		// below, regardless of the error path.
565		strictVerdict, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdictStrict(output)
566		perAC := parseReviewACTable(output)
567		rationale := strings.TrimSpace(extractReviewRationale(output, perAC))
568		if len(perAC) == 0 {
569			perAC = synthesizeReviewACsFromRationale(strictVerdict, rationale)
570		}
571		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
572		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
573			Verdict:         strictVerdict,
574			Rationale:       rationale,
575			PerAC:           perAC,
576			RawOutput:       output,
577			ReviewerHarness: actualHarness,
578			ReviewerModel:   actualModel,
579			SessionID:       sessionID,
580			BaseRev:         baseRev,
581			ResultRev:       resultRev,
582			ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
583			DurationMS:      durationMS,
584		}
585		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
586			Harness:      actualHarness,
587			Model:        actualModel,
588			SessionID:    sessionID,
589			BaseRev:      baseRev,
590			ResultRev:    resultRev,
591			Verdict:      string(strictVerdict),
592			BeadID:       beadID,
593			ExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,
594		}, reviewArtifactResult{
595			Verdict:   string(strictVerdict),
596			PerAC:     perAC,
597			Rationale: rationale,
598			Error:     reviewRes.Error,
599		})
600		if parseErr != nil {
601			// Return the review result alongside the parse error so the caller
602			// can surface the full rationale + raw output as review-error event
603			// body (loop's reviewErr path) while still having access to the
604			// reviewer text for operator forensics.
605			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
606		}
607		return reviewRes, nil
608	}

610	// dispatchReviewRun resolves how the review invocation should be executed.
611	// Resolution order matches dispatchAgentRun:
612	//  1. r.Runner (test injection seam) — used directly via Runner.Run.
613	//  2. r.Service (pre-built service) — used via RunViaServiceWith.
614	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(ProjectRoot)
615	//     and dispatch via RunViaServiceWith.
616	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runOpts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
617		if r.Runner != nil {
618			return r.Runner.Run(runOpts)
619		}
620		svc := r.Service
621		if svc == nil {
622			built, err := NewServiceFromWorkDir(r.ProjectRoot)
623			if err != nil {
624				return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: build agent service: %w", err)
625			}
626			svc = built
627		}
628		return RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, r.ProjectRoot, runOpts)
629	}

631	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
632	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
633	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
634	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
635	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
636		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
637		defer cancel()
638		args := append([]string{"-C", r.ProjectRoot, "show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
639		out, err := osexec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...).Output()
640		if err != nil {
641			return "", err
642		}
643		return string(out), nil
644	}

646	func resolveReviewBaseRev(projectRoot, resultRev string) string {
647		if resultRev == "" {
648			return ""
649		}
650		out, err := osexec.Command("git", "-C", projectRoot, "rev-parse", resultRev+"^").Output()
651		if err != nil {
652			return ""
653		}
654		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
655	}

111	func (f *CommandFactory) runBeadReview(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
112		beadID := args[0]
113		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from-rev")
114		iter, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("iter")
115		outputFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output")
116	
117		s := f.beadStore()
118		b, err := s.Get(beadID)
119		if err != nil {
120			return err
121		}
122	
123		// Resolve the commit SHA to review.
124		// Priority: --from-rev flag > closing_commit_sha on the bead.
125		rev := strings.TrimSpace(fromRev)
126		if rev == "" {
127			if sha, ok := b.Extra["closing_commit_sha"].(string); ok {
128				rev = strings.TrimSpace(sha)
129			}
130		}
131		if rev == "" {
132			return fmt.Errorf("no commit to review: use --from-rev <sha>, or close the bead with a commit SHA first (ddx bead close --commit <sha>)")
133		}
134	
135		// Project root is used for git operations and governing doc file reads.
136		projectRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
137		if projectRoot == "" {
138			projectRoot = f.WorkingDir
139		}
140	
141		// Fetch the git diff for the commit.
142		diff, err := beadReviewGitShow(projectRoot, rev)
143		if err != nil {
144			return fmt.Errorf("git show %s: %w", rev, err)
145		}
146	
147		// Resolve governing document references from the bead's spec-id field.
148		refs := agent.ResolveGoverningRefs(projectRoot, b)
149	
150		// Build and emit the review prompt.
151		var sb strings.Builder
152		buildBeadReviewPrompt(&sb, b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs)
153	
154		prompt := sb.String()
155		if outputFile != "" {
156			return os.WriteFile(outputFile, []byte(prompt), 0o644)
157		}
158		_, err = fmt.Fprint(cmd.OutOrStdout(), prompt)
159		return err
160	}

162	// beadReviewGitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for
163	// execution-evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays
164	// bounded. See agent.EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896.
165	func beadReviewGitShow(projectRoot, rev string) (string, error) {
166		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
167		defer cancel()
168		args := append([]string{"-C", projectRoot, "show", rev, "--", "."}, agent.EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
169		out, err := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", args...).Output()
170		if err != nil {
171			return "", err
172		}
173		return string(out), nil
174	}

176	// buildBeadReviewPrompt writes the complete review prompt to sb.
177	func buildBeadReviewPrompt(sb *strings.Builder, b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []agent.GoverningRef) {
178		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
179	
180		// ── Bead section ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
181		fmt.Fprintf(sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
182		fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
183	
184		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
185			fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(desc))
186		} else {
187			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
188		}
189	
190		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
191			fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(acc))
192		} else {
193			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
194		}
195	
196		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
197			fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
198		}
199	
200		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
201	
202		// ── Governing docs section ───────────────────────────────────────────────
203		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
204		if len(refs) == 0 {
205			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
206		} else {
207			for _, ref := range refs {
208				if ref.Title != "" {
209					fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
210				} else {
211					fmt.Fprintf(sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
212				}
213	
214				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
215				content, readErr := os.ReadFile(docPath)
216				if readErr != nil {
217					fmt.Fprintf(sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
218				} else {
219					fmt.Fprintf(sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", strings.TrimSpace(string(content)))
220				}
221				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
222			}
223		}
224		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
225	
226		// ── Diff section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
227		// Diff content is not XML-escaped; it is raw git show output that the
228		// reviewing agent reads as-is. The <diff> tag is a structural delimiter.
229		fmt.Fprintf(sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, strings.TrimRight(diff, "\n"))
230	
231		// ── Instructions section ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
232		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
233		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
234		fmt.Fprintf(sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", beadReviewXMLEscape(instructions))
235	
236		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
237	}

239	// beadReviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML-like attribute
240	// values and text content in the review prompt.
241	func beadReviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
242		var buf bytes.Buffer
243		_ = xml.EscapeText(&buf, []byte(s))
244		return buf.String()
245	}

260	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) Run(ctx context.Context, opts ExecuteBeadLoopOptions) (*ExecuteBeadLoopResult, error) {
261		if w.Store == nil {
262			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: store is required")
263		}
264		if w.Executor == nil {
265			return nil, fmt.Errorf("execute-bead loop: executor is required")
266		}
267	
268		now := w.Now
269		if now == nil {
270			now = time.Now
271		}
272		assignee := opts.Assignee
273		if assignee == "" {
274			assignee = "ddx"
275		}
276		noProgressCooldown := opts.NoProgressCooldown
277		if noProgressCooldown <= 0 {
278			noProgressCooldown = 6 * time.Hour
279		}
280		maxNoChangesBeforeClose := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
281		if maxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
282			maxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
283		}
284		heartbeatInterval := opts.HeartbeatInterval
285		if heartbeatInterval <= 0 {
286			heartbeatInterval = bead.HeartbeatInterval
287		}
288	
289		result := &ExecuteBeadLoopResult{}
290		attempted := make(map[string]struct{})
291	
292		emit := func(eventType string, data map[string]any) {
293			writeLoopEvent(opts.EventSink, opts.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
294		}
295	
296		emit("loop.start", map[string]any{
297			"worker_id":    opts.WorkerID,
298			"project_root": opts.ProjectRoot,
299			"harness":      opts.Harness,
300			"model":        opts.Model,
301			"session_id":   opts.SessionID,
302			"assignee":     assignee,
303			"once":         opts.Once,
304		})
305		defer func() {
306			emit("loop.end", map[string]any{
307				"attempts":            result.Attempts,
308				"successes":           result.Successes,
309				"failures":            result.Failures,
310				"last_failure_status": result.LastFailureStatus,
311			})
312		}()
313	
314		for {
315			// Respect context cancellation between iterations. Without this check,
316			// a Stop() request (which cancels ctx) would only take effect during
317			// the idle poll sleep — the loop would happily claim the next ready
318			// bead as soon as the current Execute returned, ignoring the cancel.
319			if err := ctx.Err(); err != nil {
320				return result, err
321			}
322	
323			candidate, ok, err := w.nextCandidate(attempted, opts.LabelFilter)
324			if err != nil {
325				return result, err
326			}
327			if !ok {
328				if result.Attempts == 0 {
329					result.NoReadyWork = true
330					if diag, ok := w.Store.(readyDiagnoser); ok {
331						if breakdown, bErr := diag.ReadyExecutionBreakdown(); bErr == nil {
332							result.NoReadyWorkDetail = NoReadyWorkBreakdown{
333								SkippedEpics:       breakdown.SkippedEpics,
334								SkippedOnCooldown:  breakdown.SkippedOnCooldown,
335								SkippedNotEligible: breakdown.SkippedNotEligible,
336								SkippedSuperseded:  breakdown.SkippedSuperseded,
337								NextRetryAfter:     breakdown.NextRetryAfter,
338							}
339						}
340					}
341				}
342				if opts.PollInterval <= 0 {
343					return result, nil
344				}
345				if err := sleepWithContext(ctx, opts.PollInterval); err != nil {
346					return result, err
347				}
348				continue
349			}
350	
351			attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}
352	
353			// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.
354			// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop
355			// continues (ctx is not cancelled).
356			if opts.PreClaimHook != nil {
357				if hookErr := opts.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {
358					if opts.Log != nil {
359						_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(opts.Log, "pre-claim hook: %v (skipping %s)\n", hookErr, candidate.ID)
360					}
361					emit("preclaim.skipped", map[string]any{
362						"bead_id": candidate.ID,
363						"reason":  hookErr.Error(),
364					})
365					continue
366				}
367			}
368	
369			if err := w.Store.Claim(candidate.ID, assignee); err != nil {
370				continue
371			}
372

```go
func RunViaServiceWith(ctx context.Context, svc agentlib.DdxAgent, workDir string, opts RunOptions) (*Result, error) {
    req := agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest{
        Prompt:          promptText,
        Model:           opts.Model,
        Provider:        opts.Provider,
        Harness:         harness,
        ModelRef:        opts.ModelRef,
        Reasoning:       agentlib.Reasoning(opts.Effort),
        Permissions:     opts.Permissions,
        WorkDir:         wd,
        Timeout:         wall,
        IdleTimeout:     idle,
        ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout,
        SessionLogDir:   logDir,
        Metadata:        opts.Correlation,
    }
    events, err := svc.Execute(cancelCtx, req)
}
```

**Harness Resolution** (`cli/internal/agent/types.go`):

```go
type RunOptions struct {
    Context       context.Context
    Harness       string  // e.g., "claude", "codex"
    Prompt        string
    PromptFile    string
    Model         string
    Provider      string  // explicit provider override
    ModelRef      string  // catalog model-ref (e.g., "code-medium")
    Effort        string
    Timeout       time.Duration
    WallClock     time.Duration
    WorkDir       string
    Permissions   string
    SessionLogDir string
}
```

**Key Invocation Patterns:**
- **Virtual harness**: Routed via `runFixtureHarnessViaRunner()` (DDx-owned record/replay keyed by prompt hash)
- **Script harness**: Routed via `runFixtureHarnessViaRunner()` (DDx-owned directive file reader)
- **Claude/Codex/Other**: Routed through `agentlib.DdxAgent.Execute()` service interface
  - No explicit "no-tool" mode flag found (tool use is controlled by the harness/provider)
  - Permissions field controls safety scope (safe, supervised, unrestricted)

---

### 5. GraphQL Mutations for Review Sessions

**NOTE: No reviewSessionStart/reviewSessionRespond mutations exist in the GraphQL schema.**

The GraphQL schema (`cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql`, 2,715 lines) defines:
- 10+ resolver files (resolver_*.go)
- No `ReviewSession` type or related mutations
- No `reviewSessionStart`, `reviewSessionRespond` operations

Review is **not** surfaced through GraphQL as an interactive session. Instead:
- Reviews are triggered post-merge by the execute-loop (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`, line 459)
- The reviewer harness is dispatched directly via `agentlib.DdxAgent.Execute()`
- Results are stored as bead events with artifact sidecars

**Available GraphQL Mutations** (relevant to execution):
```graphql
mutation {
    beadCreate(input: BeadInput!): Bead!
    beadUpdate(id: ID!, input: BeadUpdateInput!): Bead!
    beadClaim(id: ID!, assignee: String!): Bead!
    beadUnclaim(id: ID!): Bead!
    beadReopen(id: ID!): Bead!
    beadClose(id: ID!, reason: String): Bead!
    
    # Worker lifecycle (not review-specific)
    workerDispatch(kind: String!, projectId: String!, args: String): WorkerDispatchResult!
    startWorker(input: StartWorkerInput!): WorkerDispatchResult!
    stopWorker(id: ID!): WorkerLifecycleResult!
}
```

---

### 6. Key File Paths Summary

**Review Core:**
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go` (683 lines) - ReviewVerdict, ReviewResult, DefaultBeadReviewer
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go` (24KB) - Review tests
- `/cli/internal/agent/reviewer_stream_artifact.go` (145 lines) - Artifact persistence logic
- `/cli/cmd/bead_review.go` (246 lines) - CLI prompt generation for review

**Execute Loop & Dispatch:**
- `/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` (950 lines) - Main worker loop + review invocation
- `/cli/internal/agent/service_run.go` (~150 lines) - Service-based harness dispatch
- `/cli/internal/agent/types.go` (100 lines) - RunOptions, Result, Config types

**Server & GraphQL:**
- `/cli/internal/server/server.go` (124KB)
- `/cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql` (2,715 lines)
- `/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_*.go` (multiple files)

**No Dedicated Review Server:**
- No `/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_review.go`
- No `/cli/internal/review/` package
- No `.ddx/reviews/` session data store

---

### 7. Review Execution Flow (Complete)

1. **Pre-merge:** Agent runs via `ExecuteBead` → produces commit SHA
2. **Post-merge:** Execute-loop detects successful status
3. **Review Check:**
   - Skip if: `opts.NoReview || w.Reviewer == nil || HasBeadLabel(labels, "review:skip")`
4. **ReviewBead Invocation:**
   ```go
   reviewRes, reviewErr := w.Reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, candidate.ID, report.ResultRev, report.Harness, report.Model)
   ```
5. **Review Harness Dispatch:** (in `DefaultBeadReviewer.ReviewBead()`)
   - Fetch bead metadata + git diff
   - Build XML-structured prompt (bead section + governing docs + diff + instructions)
   - Create artifact bundle (prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json)
   - Resolve reviewer harness (defaults to "claude") + model (defaults to TierSmart)
   - Dispatch via `agentlib.DdxAgent.Execute()` with prompt
6. **Parse Output:**
   - Extract verdict (APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK) via regex: `^#{1,4}\s+Verdict:\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\s*$`
   - Extract AC grades from markdown table
   - Extract rationale from "Summary" or "Findings" sections
7. **Verdict Handling:**
   - **APPROVE:** Record event, close bead
   - **REQUEST_CHANGES:** Record event, reopen with findings in notes
   - **BLOCK:** Record event, reopen with "REVIEW:BLOCK" marker + rationale
8. **Artifact Persistence:**
   - Full reviewer output → `.ddx/executions/<attemptID>/reviewer-stream.log`
   - Event body (512 byte max) → bead event with verdict summary + artifact path reference

277	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is

289	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
290		caps := opts.Caps
291		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
292			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
293		}
294	
295		var sb strings.Builder
296		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
297	
298		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
299	
300		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
301		beadStart := sb.Len()
302		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
303		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
304	
305		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
306			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
307		} else {
308			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
309		}
310	
311		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
312			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
313		} else {
314			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
315		}
316	
317		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
318			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
319		}
320	
321		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
322			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
323		}
324	
325		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
326		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
327			Name:          "bead",
328			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
329			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
330		})
331	
332		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
333		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
334		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
335			invStart := sb.Len()
336			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
337			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
338			for _, f := range allFiles {
339				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
340				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
341			}
342			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
343			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
344				Name:          "changed-files",
345				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
346				SelectedItems: paths,
347			})
348		}
349	
350		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
351		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
352		if len(refs) == 0 {
353			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
354		} else {
355			for _, ref := range refs {
356				if ref.Title != "" {
357					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
358				} else {
359					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
360				}
361				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
362				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
363				if readErr != nil {
364					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
365					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
366						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
367						TruncationReason: "read_error",
368						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
369					})
370				} else {
371					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
372					if truncated {
373						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
374					}
375					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
376					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
377						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
378						BytesIncluded: len(content),
379						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
380					}
381					if truncated {
382						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
383						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
384					}
385					sections = append(sections, sec)
386				}
387				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
388			}
389		}
390		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
391	
392		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
393		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
394		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
395		diffSection.Name = "diff"
396		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
397		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
398	
399		if includeProse {
400			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
401			if proseErr != nil {
402				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
403				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
404				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
405				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
406					Name:          "prose-review",
407					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
408				})
409			} else {
410				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
411				sb.WriteString("\n")
412				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
413			}
414		}
415	
416		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
417		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
418		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
419		if includeProse {
420			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
421		}
422		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
423	
424		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
425	
426		out := sb.String()
427		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
428			Name:    "review-prompt",
429			Content: out,
430		}}, len(out))
431		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
432			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
433			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
434			Sections: sections,
435		}
436	}

438	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
439		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
440		if err != nil {
441			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
442		}
443	
444		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
445		if err != nil {
446			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
447		}
448		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
449		if err != nil {
450			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
451		}
452	
453		var src strings.Builder
454		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
455			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
456		}
457		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
458			src.WriteString(desc)
459			src.WriteString("\n\n")
460		}
461		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
462			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
463			src.WriteString(acc)
464			src.WriteString("\n\n")
465		}
466		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
467			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
468			src.WriteString(notes)
469			src.WriteString("\n")
470		}
471	
472		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
473		var sb strings.Builder
474		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
475		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
476		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
477		if len(findings) == 0 {
478			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
479		} else {
480			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
481			for _, finding := range findings {
482				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
483				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
484				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
485				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
486			}
487			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
488		}
489		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
490	
491		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
492			Name:          "prose-review",
493			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
494		}
495		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
496		return sb.String(), section, nil
497	}

499	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
500	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
501	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
502	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
503	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
504	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
505	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
506		if len(files) == 0 {
507			return originalDiff
508		}
509		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
510			if f.Path == "" {
511				return 3
512			}
513			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
514				return 0
515			}
516			for _, r := range refs {
517				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
518					return 1
519				}
520			}
521			return 2
522		}
523		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
524		copy(ordered, files)
525		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
526	
527		var b2 strings.Builder
528		for _, f := range ordered {
529			body := f.Body
530			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
531				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
532			}
533			b2.WriteString(body)
534		}
535		return b2.String()
536	}
537	
538	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
539	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
540	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
541	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
542	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
543		var b strings.Builder
544		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
545		b.WriteString(f.Path)
546		b.WriteString(" b/")
547		b.WriteString(f.Path)
548		b.WriteString("\n")
549		if f.Stat != "" {
550			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
551			b.WriteString("\n")
552		}
553		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
554			b.WriteString(h)
555			b.WriteString("\n")
556		}
557		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
558		return b.String()
559	}

561	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
562	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
563	// exact symbolic resolution).
564	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
565		if text == "" || p == "" {
566			return false
567		}
568		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
569			return true
570		}
571		base := path.Base(p)
572		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
573	}
574	
575	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
576	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
577	// documents.
578	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
579		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
580			return false
581		}
582		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
583		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
584			return true
585		}
586		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
587			return true
588		}
589		return false
590	}

592	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
593	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
594		var buf bytes.Buffer
595		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
596			"&", "&",
597			"<", "<",
598			">", ">",
599		).Replace(s))
600		return buf.String()
601	}

603	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
604	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
605	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
606	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
607		ProjectRoot string
608		BeadStore   BeadReader
609		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
610		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
611		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
612		Service agentlib.FizeauService
613		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
614		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
615		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
616		Runner AgentRunner
617		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
618		// When Harness is empty, the reviewer follows the implementation harness;
619		// when both are empty, dispatch is left to the agent service.
620		Harness string
621		Model   string
622		// ModelRef is retained for tests/legacy callers that explicitly want an
623		// upstream model-ref override. Default reviewer routing uses fizeau profiles.
624		ModelRef string
625		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
626		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
627		// applies.
628		Caps evidence.Caps
629		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
630		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
631		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
632		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
633		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
634	}

636	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
637	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It sets the reviewer harness/profile,
638	// attaches the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid),
639	// and derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
640	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
641	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
642	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
643	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string) AgentRunRuntime {
644		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
645	}
646	
647	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
648	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
649	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
650	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
651	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
652	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProfile string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
653		correlation := map[string]string{}
654		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
655			correlation[k] = v
656		}
657		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
658		if meta.GroupID != "" {
659			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
660			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
661		}
662		if impl.Harness != "" {
663			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
664		}
665		if impl.Provider != "" {
666			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
667		}
668		if impl.Model != "" {
669			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
670		}
671		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
672			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
673		}
674		minPower := 0
675		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
676			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
677		}
678		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
679		return AgentRunRuntime{
680			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
681			ProfileOverride:     reviewerProfile,
682			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
683			Correlation:         correlation,
684			Role:                "reviewer",
685			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
686			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
687			ClearRoutingPins:    true,
688			ClearProfile:        true,
689			ClearMaxPower:       true,
690		}
691	}

697	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) reviewerDispatchProfile(ctx context.Context, impl ImplementerRouting) reviewerDispatchProfile {
698		floor := 0
699		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
700			floor = impl.ActualPower + 1
701		}
702		if r.Runner != nil {
703			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
704		}
705		selectedSvc := r.Service
706		if selectedSvc == nil {
707			factory := serviceRunFactory
708			if factory == nil {
709				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
710			}
711			built, err := factory(r.ProjectRoot)
712			if err != nil {
713				return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
714			}
715			selectedSvc = built
716		}
717		snap, err := LoadProfileSnapshot(ctx, selectedSvc)
718		if err != nil {
719			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
720		}
721		name := ""
722		if floor > 0 {
723			name = SelectStrongestProfileAbove(snap, floor)
724		} else {
725			name = selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap)
726		}
727		if name == "" {
728			return reviewerDispatchProfile{MinPower: floor}
729		}
730		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
731			if profile.Name == name {
732				return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: profile.MinPower}
733			}
734		}
735		return reviewerDispatchProfile{Name: name, MinPower: floor}
736	}

738	func selectSmartReviewerProfile(snap ProfileSnapshot) string {
739		for _, profile := range snap.Profiles {
740			if profile.Name == "smart" && profileHasAvailableModel(profile, snap.Models) {
741				return profile.Name
742			}
743		}
744		return SelectStrongestProfile(snap)
745	}

747	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) applyExplicitReviewerPins(runtime *AgentRunRuntime) string {

749			return ""
750		}
751		if r.Model != "" {

753			return r.Model
754		}
755		if r.ModelRef != "" {
756			runtime.ModelRefOverride = r.ModelRef
757			return r.ModelRef
758		}
759		return runtime.ProfileOverride

762	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
763		if len(correlation) == 0 {
764			return ""
765		}
766		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
767		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
768			parts = append(parts, beadID)
769		}
770		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
771			parts = append(parts, groupID)
772		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
773			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
774		}
775		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
776			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
777		}
778		if len(parts) == 0 {
779			return ""
780		}
781		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
782	}

784	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
785	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
786	// operators can see exactly which routes converged.
787	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int) string {
788		var b strings.Builder
789		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
790		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
791		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
792		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
793		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
794		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
795		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
796		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
797		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
798		return b.String()
799	}

811	// Review implements CandidateReviewer for the pre-land candidate-cycle path.
812	// It reviews the full base_rev..candidate_rev range and dispatches the reviewer
813	// inside the still-live attempt worktree.

816		if reviewer.ProjectRoot == "" {

820			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: project_root required")
821		}
822		beadID := candidate.Report.BeadID
823		if beadID == "" {
824			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: bead_id required")
825		}
826		diff, err := reviewer.gitDiff(candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev)
827		if err != nil {
828			return CandidateReviewResult{}, fmt.Errorf("candidate reviewer: git diff %s..%s: %w", candidate.Report.BaseRev, candidate.Report.ResultRev, err)
829		}
830		workDir := candidate.WorktreePath
831		if workDir == "" {
832			workDir = reviewer.ProjectRoot
833		}
834		impl := ImplementerRouting{
835			Harness:     candidate.Report.Harness,
836			Provider:    candidate.Report.Provider,
837			Model:       candidate.Report.Model,
838			ActualPower: candidate.Report.ActualPower,
839			Correlation: map[string]string{
840				"bead_id":       beadID,
841				"attempt_id":    candidate.Report.AttemptID,
842				"session_id":    candidate.Report.SessionID,
843				"result_rev":    candidate.Report.ResultRev,
844				"candidate_ref": candidate.Report.CandidateRef,
845				"cycle_index":   fmt.Sprintf("%d", candidate.CycleIndex),
846			},
847		}
848		group, groupErr := reviewer.reviewGroupWithDiff(ctx, beadID, candidate.Report.ResultRev, impl, diff, workDir)
849		review, reduceErr := reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group)
850		if review == nil {
851			if reduceErr != nil {
852				return CandidateReviewResult{}, reduceErr
853			}
854			return CandidateReviewResult{}, groupErr
855		}
856		out := CandidateReviewResult{
857			Verdict:        string(review.Verdict),
858			Rationale:      review.Rationale,
859			PerAC:          append([]ReviewAC(nil), review.PerAC...),
860			Findings:       append([]Finding(nil), review.Findings...),
861			Classification: ClassifyReviewFindings(review).Class,
862		}
863		if reduceErr != nil {
864			return out, reduceErr
865		}
866		return out, groupErr
867	}

18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.

26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.

33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can override one knob
34	// for a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {

41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride

44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}

52		if svc == nil {

1	package agent
2	
3	import "fmt"
4	
5	// PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer is the PermissionsOverride value stamped on
6	// every reviewer AgentRunRuntime by BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest. The
7	// dispatch boundary (RunWithConfigViaService) validates that the resolved
8	// harness can honour this constraint; harnesses that cannot are rejected with
9	// ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError rather than silently running unrestricted.
10	const PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer = "readonly"
11	
12	// ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError is returned when a reviewer dispatch is
13	// attempted with PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer against a harness that cannot
14	// enforce read-only tool access. Callers must surface this as a review-error
15	// class; they must not fall back to unrestricted dispatch.
16	type ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError struct {
17		Harness string
18	}
19	
20	func (e *ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError) Error() string {
21		return fmt.Sprintf(
22			"reviewer read-only enforcement: harness %q cannot enforce read-only tool access; dispatch rejected",
23			e.Harness,
24		)
25	}
26	
27	// readOnlyCapableHarnesses enumerates harnesses known to honour
28	// PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer. All others fail closed.
29	var readOnlyCapableHarnesses = map[string]bool{
30		"claude":  true, // Claude Code permission system enforces tool restrictions.
31		"agent":   true, // Forwarded to fizeau; fizeau honours the permissions field.
32		"virtual": true, // Content-addressed replay; no mutation possible by construction.
33	}
34	
35	// ValidateReadOnlyReviewerDispatch returns ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError when
36	// permissions equals PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer and harness is absent from
37	// readOnlyCapableHarnesses. Returns nil when enforcement is not required or
38	// the harness is known capable.
39	func ValidateReadOnlyReviewerDispatch(harness, permissions string) error {
40		if permissions != PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer {
41			return nil
42		}
43		if readOnlyCapableHarnesses[harness] {
44			return nil
45		}
46		return &ReviewReadOnlyEnforcementError{Harness: harness}
47	}

429		DefaultHarness = "codex"
430		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
431		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
432		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
433		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
434		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
435		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
436		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
437		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
438		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
439		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
440		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
441		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
442		// ToolCallTimeout is a per-tool-call sanity cap: if a single tool_call
443		// event has no matching tool_result within this window, the drain cancels
444		// the execution. This is NOT for loop detection (see loopDetector in
445		// agent_runner_service.go) — it catches individually hung subprocesses
446		// (e.g., network deadlock in git push) that would otherwise absorb
447		// unlimited wall-clock without surfacing.
448		ToolCallTimeout = 1800000 // 30 minutes
449		DefaultLogDir   = ".ddx/agent-logs"
450	)
451	
452	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
453	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
454	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
455	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
456	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
457		if configured == "" {
458			configured = DefaultLogDir
459		}
460		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
461			return configured
462		}
463		if projectRoot == "" {
464			return configured
465		}
466		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
467	}
